key_insight: Droplet fusion and shape recovery are both capillary-driven, but viscoelastic stress relaxation makes them fundamentally different relaxation processes with distinct timescales and stages.
engineering_relevance: For biomolecular condensates and soft-matter systems, purely viscous models can mispredict recovery and coalescence, so design and interpretation should account for Deborah-number-dependent viscoelastic effects and external-fluid dissipation.
method: Analytical small-deformation theory for shape recovery
Axisymmetric finite-element simulations using the Oldroyd-B constitutive model
Comparison of Newtonian and viscoelastic droplets under matched conditions
evidence_signal: The study combines theory and simulation to show single- or multi-exponential shape recovery, multistage fusion dynamics, and deviations from stretched-exponential fits in viscoelastic regimes.
watch_item: Oldroyd-B modeling and stretched-exponential fits may not capture all material behaviors, especially when strong heterogeneity or non-Oldroyd viscoelasticity is present.
paper_title: Droplet Fusion as a Relaxation Process: Comparison with Shape Recovery of Newtonian and Viscoelastic Droplets
